briefdescription.para: Input Operations.
Output Operations.
This macro reads a value from a DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit read is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is read from the register. The most significant data will be read as 0.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file).

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XDpTxSs_ReadReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: This macro writes a value to a DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem register.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the register offset of the register (defined at the top of this file) to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDpTxSs_WriteReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: A HPD event interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
A HPD pulse interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Lane count change interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Link rate change interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Pre-emphasis and voltage swing change interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Set MSA immediate change change interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Driver's internal HPD event interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Driver's HPD pulse interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Driver's extended packet transmit done interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
Driver's extended packet transmit done interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
A Vsync interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
FFE Preset coefficients change interrupt type for DisplayPort core.
These constants specify different types of handler and used to differentiate interrupt requests from sub-cores.
No content protection.
HDCP 1X.
HDCP 2.2.
Both HDCP 1.4 and 2.2.
These constants specify the HDCP protection schemes.
HDCP Stream Type 0.
HDCP Stream Type 1.
These constants specify HDCP repeater content stream management type.
Preferred timing (Options bit 7 set)
Native timing (Options bit 6 set, bit 7 clear)
Other timings (both bits clear)
DisplayID Type VII timing categories for priority-based parsing.
This function reports list of cores included in DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function computes multi-stream attribute and populates frame rate, pixel clock and so on.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the stream number for which the MSA values will be computed for.

briefdescription.para: This function checks whether RX device in multi-stream (MST) / Single Stream transport mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if DisplayPort TX initialization failed or RX device is not connected.
XST_SUCCESS if RX device check is successful.

briefdescription.para: This function configures DisplayPort TX subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if all sub-cores configuration failed.
XST_SUCCESS if all sub-cores configuration was successful.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called prior to using the core. Initialization of the core includes setting up the instance data and ensuring the hardware is in a quiescent state.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
points to the configuration structure associated with the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.
is the base address of the device. If address translation is being used, then this parameter must reflect the virtual base address. Otherwise, the physical address should be used.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if sub-core not found.
XST_FAILURE if sub-core initialization failed.
XST_SUCCESS if XDpTxSs_CfgInitialize successful.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem including all sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem including all sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if DP TX Subsystem and its included sub-cores configured successfully.
XST_FAILURE, otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function starts the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem with custom multi-stream attributes (MSA)including all sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the structure that will be used to copy the main stream attributes from (into InstancePtr->DpPtr->TxInstance.MsaConfig).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if DP TX Subsystem and its included sub-cores configured successfully.
XST_FAILURE, otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function disables the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function enables special timing mode for BS equal timing.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function disables special timing mode for BS equal timing.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the bits per color value of the video stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

is the new number of bits per color that needs to be set.
6 = XVIDC_BPC_6,
8 = XVIDC_BPC_8,
10 = XVIDC_BPC_10,
12 = XVIDC_BPC_12,
16 = XVIDC_BPC_16,

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if bits per color set successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if bits per color set failed.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the MSA values for the specified video format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is the structure that will be used to copy the main stream attributes into (from InstancePtr->DpPtr->TxInstance.MsaConfig).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if MSA values read successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if MSA values read failed.

simplesect.para: Refer xvidc.h for enumerated standard video modes.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the MSA values for the specified video format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is one of the enumerated standard video modes that is used to determine the MSA values to be used.
number

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if video mode set successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if video mode set failed.

simplesect.para: Refer xvidc.h for enumerated standard video modes.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the VideoStream structure for the specified video format.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is one of the enumerated standard video modes that is used to determine the MSA values to be used.
Streamnumber to set the video params

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if video mode set successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if video mode set failed.

simplesect.para: Refer xvidc.h for enumerated standard video modes.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the standard display mode.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is one of the enumerated standard video modes that is used to determine the MSA values to be used.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if video mode set successfully.
XST_FAILURE, if video mode set failed.

simplesect.para: Refer xvidc.h for enumerated standard video modes.

briefdescription.para: This function overrides the Polarity for Horizontal and Vertical Sync signals.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is the stream number for which to change the Polarity.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the data rate to be used by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new lane rate was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

simplesect.para: Maximum supported link rate is used if given link rate is greater than the maximum supported link rate.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the Repeater mode to be used by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

is the rate at which link needs to be driven.
XDP_TX_LTTPR_TRANSPARENT
XDP_TX_LTTPR_NON_TRANSPARENT

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new lane rate was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the data rate to be used by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

is the rate at which link needs to be driven.
XDPTXSS_LINK_BW_SET_162GBPS = 0x06(for a 1.62 Gbps data rate)
XDPTXSS_LINK_BW_SET_270GBPS = 0x0A(for a 2.70 Gbps data rate)
XDPTXSS_LINK_BW_SET_540GBPS = 0x14(for a 5.40 Gbps data rate)

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new lane rate was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

simplesect.para: Maximum supported link rate is used if given link rate is greater than the maximum supported link rate.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the number of lanes to be used by DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

is the number of lanes to be used.
1 = XDPTXSS_LANE_COUNT_SET_1
2 = XDPTXSS_LANE_COUNT_SET_2
4 = XDPTXSS_LANE_COUNT_SET_4

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if setting the new lane count was successful.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

simplesect.para: Maximum supported lane count is used if given lane count is greater than the maximum supported lane count.

briefdescription.para: This function sets transport mode (SST/MST).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

specifies the type of transport mode that will be set.
0 = Single-Stream Transport mode,
1 = Multi-Stream Transport mode,

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS, if transport mode is set successfully to either MST or SST when RX device is MST and mode is less than or equal to supported mode.
XST_FAILURE, if setting to already set mode or mode is greater than supported mode.

simplesect.para: Transport mode is set to either MST or SST when system is MST and RX device is MST capable.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the RX device's capabilities from the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort Configuration Data was read successfully.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves the Sink device's capabilities from the RX device's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
Downstream Capabilities.
Downstream Extended Capabilities.
Maximum Link Rate supported by Sink.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the DisplayPort Configuration Data was read successfully.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves an immediately connected RX device's Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
is a pointer to the Edid buffer to save to.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the I2C transactions to read the EDID were successful.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the EDID read request timed out.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function retrieves a remote RX device's Extended Display Identification Data (EDID) structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the Sink ID in the sink list within the range [0 to 3].
is a pointer to the Edid buffer to save to.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the I2C transactions to read the EDID were successful.
XST_ERROR_COUNT_MAX if the EDID read request timed out.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if there is a connected RX device to DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if there is a connection.
FALSE if there is no connection.

briefdescription.para: This function checks if the receiver's DisplayPort Configuration Data (DPCD) indicates the receiver has achieved and maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment for all lanes currently in use.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device has maintained clock recovery, channel equalization, symbol lock, and interlane alignment.
XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if no RX device is connected.
XST_FAILURE otherwise.

briefdescription.para: This function configures the number of pixels output through the user data interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
is the user pixel width to be configured.
is the stream number.

briefdescription.para: This function determines whether downstream RX device is MST/SST capable.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device is MST enabled.
XST_FAILURE if the RX device is SST enabled.

briefdescription.para: This function returns number of downstream MST sink devices enumerated and available for payload allocation .

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: Number of MST sinks to be enabled for payload allocation

briefdescription.para: This function will check if the immediate downstream RX device capable of receiving colorimetry information through VSC extended SDP packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the RX device is capable of VSC extended packet.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the RX device does not capable of VSC extended packet.

briefdescription.para: This function decodes the extended packet payload data bytes and fills up the driver extended packet structure members.

detaileddescription.para: And also copies extended packet header information to the driver structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is a driver extended packet structure

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if VSC extended packet is set.
XST_FAILURE if VSC extended packet is not set.

briefdescription.para: This function Enable / Disable the mechanism of sending colorimetry information through VSC extended packet.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.

specifies to enable the flag
0 = Disable the sending colorimetry through VSC packet else enable Enable the sending colorimetry through VSC packet

briefdescription.para: This function sets software switch that specify whether or not a redriver exits on the DisplayPort output path.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
establishes that a redriver exists in the DisplayPort output path. 1 = Set redriver in the DisplayPort output path. 0 = Unset redriver in the DisplayPort output path.

simplesect.para: Set the redriver in the DisplayPort output path before starting the training.

briefdescription.para: This function sends audio infoframe and configures audio parameters including MAUD/NAUD values based on the current link rate.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is a pointer to the audio infoframe structure.

simplesect.para:
This function performs the following operations:
Disables audio
Configures the audio infoframe packet
Calculates and sets MAUD/NAUD based on link rate
Enables Audio

briefdescription.para: This function returns a reference to an XDptxss_Config structure based on the core id, DeviceId.

detaileddescription.para: The return value will refer to an entry in the device configuration table defined in the xdptxss_g.c file.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique core ID of the XDpTxSs core for the lookup operation.

simplesect.para: XDptxss_LookupConfig returns a reference to a config record in the configuration table (in xdptxss_g.c) corresponding to DeviceId, or NULL if no match is found.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the Index number of config table using BaseAddress.

parameterdescription.para: address of the instance

simplesect.para: Index number of the config table

briefdescription.para: This function reports list of sub-cores included in DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the link status, selected resolution, bits per color and link/lane count symbol error.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the current main stream attributes from the DisplayPort TX core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the capabilities of the DisplayPort sink.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the current Dual Splitter information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the current VTC timing information.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function prints the debug display info of the HDCP interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: This function performs self test on DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem sub-cores.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if self test passed.
XST_FAILURE if self test failed.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the DisplayPort TX core operating in TX mode.

detaileddescription.para: The application is responsible for connecting this function to the interrupt system. Application beyond this driver is also responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XDpTxSs_SetCallBack() during initialization phase.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function HandlerType ---------------------------------------- ------------------------------------ (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_HPD_EVENT) XDP_TX_HANDLER_HPDEVENT (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_HPD_PULSE) XDP_TX_HANDLER_HPDPULSE (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_LANE_COUNT_CHG) XDP_TX_HANDLER_LANECNTCHANGE (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_LINK_RATE_CHG) XDP_TX_HANDLER_LINKRATECHANGE (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_PE_VS_ADJUST) XDP_TX_HANDLER_PEVSADJUST (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_EXT_PKT_EVENT) XDP_TX_HANDLER_EXTPKT_TXD (XDPTXSS_DRV_HANDLER_DP_EXT_PKT_EVENT) XDP_TX_HANDLER_DRV_EXTPKT_TXD (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_VSYNC) XDP_TX_HANDLER_VSYNC (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_HDCP_RPTR_EXCHG) XHdcp1x_SetCallBack (XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_SET_MSA) XDP_TX_HANDLER_SETMSA

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
specifies the type of handler.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if callback function installed successfully.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: This function installs a custom delay/sleep function to be used by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is the address to the callback function.
is the user data item (microseconds to delay) that will be passed to the custom sleep/delay function when it is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function is process some DP driver read as per DP spec on HPD EVENT.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function is process some DP driver read as per DP spec on HPD Pulse.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function writes the pixel encoding format and colorimetry formats in to the extended packet registers at DP TX offset addresses "0x0330 to 0x0350".

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the full EDID (base block + all extensions) from the connected monitor, validates header and checksum, and stores it in the AVCaps structure's embedded buffer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.
is a pointer to XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure where EDID will be stored in AVCaps->EdidBuffer and extension count in AVCaps->extensions_count.

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if all EDID blocks read and Base block validated successfully. XST_FAILURE if instance/structure pointer invalid, monitor not connected, EDID read fails, or EDID header/checksum invalid.
This function validates base EDID header and checksum. Extension count is limited to MAX_EXTENSIONS_SUPPORTED. Each EDID block is 128 bytes.

briefdescription.para: This function parses complete Audio/Video capabilities and timings from embedded EDID buffer by aggregating data from base EDID, CTA-861, and DisplayID extension blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure with EDID already loaded in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. On return, AVCaps->checksum_failures will contain the count of extension blocks with checksum errors (0 if all blocks valid).

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if all blocks are parsed successfully XST_FAILURE if any error is encountered

EDID must be loaded via XDpTxSs_get_full_edid() before calling. Maximum SAD_count is limited to MAX_SAD_COUNT. Maximum timing count is limited to EDID_MAX_TIMINGS. CTA-861 blocks are optional (success returned if none found). DisplayID blocks are optional (success returned if none found). Speaker allocation is OR'd across all CTA-861 blocks. If arrays fill up, parsing continues but additional entries ignored. Video timings are parsed by calling XDpTxSs_GetVideoTimingsFromEdid() internally and stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] in priority order. Parse statistics from both CTA-861 and DisplayID blocks are accumulated and reported in the summary.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam *Timing, u16 HActive, u16 VActive, u8 RefreshRate)
memberdef.name: XDpTxSs_FillEstablishedTiming

briefdescription.para: Helper function to fill established timing with known values and zero-fill unavailable parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure to populate.
is the horizontal active pixels.
is the vertical active lines.
is the refresh rate in Hz.

memberdef.argsstring: (XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam *Timing, u16 HActive, u16 VActive, u8 AspectRatio, u8 RefreshRate)
memberdef.name: XDpTxSs_FillStandardTiming

briefdescription.para: Helper function to fill standard timing with known values and zero-fill unavailable parameters.

parameterdescription.para: is the XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure to populate.
is the horizontal active pixels.
is the vertical active lines.
is the aspect ratio code (0-3).
is the refresh rate in Hz.

briefdescription.para: This function converts XV_VidC_TimingParam to XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam format.

parameterdescription.para: is the source timing parameter structure.
is the destination XDpTxSs_VideoTimingParam structure.

briefdescription.para: This function validates all DisplayID 2.0 extension blocks by checking checksums.

detaileddescription.para: Called once to count total and invalid DisplayID blocks.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the AV capabilities structure with EDID in AVCaps->EdidBuffer.

briefdescription.para: This function parses DisplayID 2.0 extension blocks and extracts Type VII detailed timings based on the specified category filter (Preferred/Native/Remaining).

detaileddescription.para: This function assumes that blocks have been pre-validated.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the AV capabilities structure with EDID in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. Timings will be stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] and AVCaps->TimingCount is updated with the number of timings added.

specifies which timing category to extract:
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_PREFERRED: Timings with Preferred flag (Options bit 7)
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_NATIVE: Timings with Native flag (Options bit 6, Preferred clear)
DISPLAYID_TYPE7_REMAINING: All other timings (both flags clear)

simplesect.para: XST_SUCCESS if parsing completed. XST_FAILURE if AVCaps pointer is invalid.
This function does NOT validate checksums - caller must validate first. DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Timing Descriptor Structure (20 bytes)

briefdescription.para: This function extracts video timings from an EDID structure and populates the timing list within an AV capabilities structure in priority order.

detaileddescription.para: Index 0 contains the highest priority timing (preferred timing) from the sink.

Priority order (EdidTimingPriority enum):
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_PREFERRED - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Preferred
PRIORITY_BASE_DTD1_PREFERRED - Base EDID DTD #1 (EDID spec mandated preferred)
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_NATIVE - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII Native
PRIORITY_DISPLAYID_DTD_OTHER - DisplayID 2.0 Type VII (remaining DTDs)
PRIORITY_BASE_DTD_OTHER - Base EDID DTD #2-4
PRIORITY_STANDARD_TIMINGS - Base EDID Standard Timings
PRIORITY_ESTABLISHED_TIMINGS - Base EDID Established Timings

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs_Sink_AV_Capabilities structure with EDID loaded in AVCaps->EdidBuffer. Timings are stored in AVCaps->TimingList[] (max EDID_MAX_TIMINGS) and count in AVCaps->TimingCount.

simplesect.para: This function is typically called internally by XDpTxSs_get_sink_AV_Capabilities(). The caller must ensure the base EDID header and checksum have been validated before calling. The AVCaps structure must be allocated by the caller.

briefdescription.para: This function prints debug information about a video timing.

parameterdescription.para: is the video timing structure to print.
is the index number to display.

briefdescription.para: Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
Enhanced frame enable for stream 1.
Enhanced frame enable for stream 2.
Training pattern for stream 3.
Training pattern for stream 4.
No stream encryption.
Encrypt all streams.
Callback type which represents a custom timer wait handler.
Main-Stream attributes.
This function enables or disables synchronous clock mode for a video stream.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDp instance.
is the stream number for which to enable or disable synchronous clock mode.
if set to 1, will enable synchronous clock mode. Otherwise, if set to 0, synchronous clock mode will be disabled.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDp_TxCfgMsaEnSynchClkMode(XDpTxSs *InstancePtr, u8 Stream, u8 Enable)

briefdescription.para: This function macro enables MST-TX audio on a given stream on the main link.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.
to be enabled audio

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XDpTxSs_Mst_AudioEnable(XDpTxSs *InstancePtr, u8 StreamId)

briefdescription.para: This function macro disables audio on a given stream on the main link.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs core instance.

briefdescription.para: Enable verbose EDID parsing debug output (0=disabled, 1=enabled)
Maximum number of timings to return.
EDID Detailed Timing Descriptor size in bytes (18 bytes)
EDID parsing limits.

detaileddescription.para: Maximum EDID extension blocks to process

briefdescription.para: Maximum Short Audio Descriptors to store.
CTA-861 extension block tag (0x02) for audio/video capability data.
DisplayID 2.0 extension block tag (0x70) for detailed timing data.
DisplayID Type VII timing descriptor tag (0x22)
CTA-861 audio data block tag (0x01) containing Short Audio Descriptors.
CTA-861 speaker allocation data block tag (0x04) for channel mapping.
Timing Source Definitions.

detaileddescription.para: Detailed Timing Descriptor

briefdescription.para: Standard Timing.
Established Timing.
Bit shift value (3) to extract audio format code from SAD byte 0.
Bitmask (0x1F) for audio format code field in SAD byte 0 (bits 3-7)
Bitmask (0x07) for maximum channel count in SAD byte 0 (bits 0-2)
Bitmask (0x7F) for supported sampling frequencies in SAD byte 1.
Bitmask (0x07) for LPCM bit depth flags in SAD byte 2 (16/20/24-bit)
Bitmask (0xFF) for maximum bitrate in SAD byte 2 (compressed formats)
Prevent circular inclusions by using protection macros.
1.62 Gbps link rate.
2.70 Gbps link rate.
5.40 Gbps link rate.
8.10 Gbps link rate.
10 Gbps link rate.
20 Gbps link rate.
13.5 Gbps link rate.
10 Gbps link rate.

detaileddescription.para: internal reference

briefdescription.para: 20 Gbps link rate.

detaileddescription.para: internal reference

briefdescription.para: 13.5 Gbps link rate.

detaileddescription.para: internal reference

briefdescription.para: Lane count of 1.
Lane count of 2.
Lane count of 4.
Masks the specified interrupt sources.
Mask HPD pulse detected interrupt.
Mask HPD event interrupt.
Mask all interrupts.
Mask for normal TX operation (HPD + IRQ enabled)
Maximum number of streams supported.
This is the main file for Xilinx DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains static initialization method for Xilinx DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem core.
This file contains self test function for the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem core.
This file contains interrupt related functions of Xilinx DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.
This header file contains identifiers and register-level core functions (or macros) that can be used to access the Xilinx DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem core.
This header file contains declarations for EDID timing parsing functions that extract video timings from EDID/DisplayID structures in priority order.
This file contains EDID timing parsing functions that extract video timings from EDID/DisplayID structures and populate it in priority order.
This file contains functions to report debug information of DisplayPort TX Subsystem sub-cores.

This file contains a minimal set of functions for the XDpTxSs driver that allow access to all of the DisplayPort Transmitter Subsystem core's functionality. Please see xdptxss.h for more details of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release. 1.00 sha 07/21/15 Renamed sub-cores functions with prefix XDpTxSs_* 2.00 sha 08/07/15 Added support for customized main stream attributes. Added HDCP instance into global sub-cores structure. 2.00 sha 09/28/15 Added HDCP and Timer Counter support. 3.0 sha 02/05/16 Added support for multiple subsystems in a design. 3.0 sha 02/19/16 Added function: XDpTxSs_ReadDownstream, XDpTxSs_HandleTimeout. Enabled HDCP in XDpTxSs_Start function. 4.1 aad 07/28/16 Enabled VTC before DPTX core enable for better image stability 4.1 als 08/08/16 Synchronize with new HDCP APIs. aad 09/06/16 Updates to support 64-bit base address 5.0 tu 07/20/17 Allowing Custom VTM in XDpTxSs_SetVidMode function. 5.0 tu 08/10/17 Adjusted BS symbol for equal timing 5.0 tu 08/11/17 Removing ceil() to remove dependency on math library. 5.0 tu 09/06/17 Set timer callback after HDCP initialization 5.0 tu 09/06/17 Added Set UserPixelWidth support on tx side 5.0 tu 09/08/17 Set HPD callbacks for HPD event and HPD pulse 5.0 jb 02/21/19 Added HDCP22 support. Made the Timer counter available for both HDCP1x and 22. 6.2 jb 02/14/20 The DP Tx subsystems assumes that the HDCP configuration is same for all the instances in multiple subsystems in the design. This driver won't support for different configuration of the subsystems. 6.4 rg 09/26/20 Added below list of APIs related to VSC extended packets XDpTxSs_CheckVscColorimetrySupport, XDpTxSs_SetVscExtendedPacket, XDpTxss_EnableVscColorimetry 6.4 rg 09/26/20 Added support for YUV420 color format 6.5 pam 03/18/26 XDpTxSs_SendAudioInfoFrame API is updated to handle Audio control registers

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release. 2.00 sha 08/07/15 Updated register offsets in debug MSA info. 2.00 sha 09/28/15 Added HDCP debug function.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.0 amd 03/18/26 Initial release

detaileddescription.para: For more information about the operation of this core see the hardware specification and documentation in the higher level driver xdptxss.h file.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release. 1.1 pam 03/18/26 Doxygen warnings removed

detaileddescription.para: Please see xdptxss.h for more details of the core.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release. 2.00 sha 08/07/15 Added new handler types: lane count, link rate, Pre-emphasis voltage swing adjust and Set MSA. 2.00 sha 09/28/15 Added HDCP and Timer Counter interrupt handlers. 3.0 sha 02/19/16 Added switch cases for XDPTXSS_HANDLER_HDCP_RPTR_DWN_STRM_RDY and XDPTXSS_HANDLER_HDCP_RPTR_EXCHG to register callback with HDCP. 4.1 als 08/08/16 Synchronize with new HDCP APIs. 5.0 tu 09/08/17 Added two interrupt handler that addresses driver's internal callback function of application DrvHpdEventHandler and DrvHpdPulseHandler 5.0 jb 02/21/19 Added HDCP22 callback handles. Made the Timer counter interrupt handler available for both HDCP1x and 22. Added switch case for 6.5 rg 09/01/20 Added switch case XDPTXSS_HANDLER_DP_EXT_PKT_EVENT to register callback with extended packet transmit done handler. 6.4 rg 09/26/20 Added driver handler function XDpTxSs_WriteVscExtPktProcess for programming the extended packet up on receiving extended packet transmission done interrupt. 6.5 pam 03/18/26 Max lane count and Max link rate parsing based on Extended receiver capability

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release. 2.00 sha 09/28/15 Added HDCP and Timer Counter self test. 4.1 tu 25/06/17 Added return values

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 01/29/15 Initial release.
